questions.question: Why does ZeRO shard optimiser state first instead of parameters?
options: Optimiser state is the largest memory term and is only touched during the step, not forward or backward
questions.explanation: For Adam in mixed precision the fp32 master plus two moments is 12P bytes per rank vs 4P for fp16 params + grads. Sharding the biggest term first wins the most memory per unit complexity.

questions.question: What is the per-step wire pattern of ZeRO stage 1?
options: One reduce_scatter on gradients plus one allgather on updated parameters
questions.explanation: Reduce_scatter delivers each rank only its gradient shard; allgather distributes the updated parameter shards back. Total bytes equal allreduce, memory is divided by N.

questions.question: Why is the per-step bandwidth the same as DDP?
options: Allreduce equals reduce_scatter plus allgather in bandwidth; ZeRO splits that into two halves on different data
questions.explanation: Ring allreduce is implemented as reduce_scatter then allgather. ZeRO does the same two operations but the allgather rotates the updated parameters, not the summed gradient.

questions.question: For a 7B model with Adam in mixed precision on 8 ranks, what is the memory drop vs vanilla DDP?
What does ZeRO-2 add over ZeRO-1?
options: Also drops the non-shard gradients after reduce_scatter, freeing per-rank gradient memory; bandwidth stays the same
questions.explanation: ZeRO-2 zeroes the non-shard gradient portion after reduce_scatter; same bandwidth as ZeRO-1 but lower gradient memory.

questions.question: Why does ZeRO require the optimiser state checkpoint to record which rank owns which shard?
options: Without per-rank ownership the saved state is unreadable at restart; resuming on the same world size needs to put the right shard back on the right rank
questions.explanation: Lesson 80 builds the sharded checkpoint manifest precisely so a ZeRO run can resume on the same topology.
